text:
The International Human Databank is a digital repository of information about people in the world. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 

Description

The International Human Databank comprises a diverse array of records, including medical records, military and academic records, DNA information, and family registers. It is known to contain information going back to at least sixty years before the Yorknew City arc events. With the IHD, it is said that one would be able to find a president’s illegitimate children in three hours. Despite this, a Mafia Hacker using the Databank for six hours could not find any records of Shalnark, Franklin, Feitan, Shizuku, Machi, or Chrollo having ever existed. 

The Databank has strong legal protections, with trying to remove records by hacking into it being considered a severe offense under international law. It is said that twenty people have been arrested for attempted murder due to trying to hack into the Database and did not receive probation, implying that they were convicted and sentenced as well. It also features robust technological safeguards, utilizing a triplicate system with two insulated computers that continuously monitor the public computer. It has been claimed that even if someone did manage to remove or rewrite some of the Databank’s data, it would be instantly corrected. 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

After the fake corpses of several Phantom Troupe members are discovered, the mafia tries to look them up on the International Human Databank, planning to kill other people affiliated with the Troupe as retribution for their attack on the underground auction.

One mafioso goes to the hacker in charge of looking up the Troupe to complain about how long he is taking. The hacker repeatedly states that the Troupe members do not exist before revealing that one could be absent from the Databank by having their birth never be officially documented. He notes that this is the situation with Meteor City. 

Trivia

Despite Shalnark being a licensed Hunter, the Databank still contained no record of his existence.
The International Human Databank does not appear and is not directly mentioned in the 2011 anime adaptation series.
